# Handoff — Ticket-Viewer Store Observability Roadmap (fresh review)

## Overview

This session researched the fragmented ticket-viewer / viewer-api planning, corrected a workspace-discovery misconception, and stood up a single high-priority tracked roadmap in the `memory-viewers` ticket store to fix six concrete ticket-viewer observability issues plus one infrastructure gap. The roadmap tracker + 8 workstream tickets exist with acceptance criteria, spec refs, effort, and dependency edges; one new spec was authored; and a durable workflow instruction note was added. Remaining work is refinement (re-scope superseded tickets, fill two spec contract sections, record validation specs), a commit, then implementation starting with the P0 leaves. Motivation for the direction: the internal stores are only observable through these viewers, so making the ticket-viewer readable/navigable and generalizing the viewer platform is the fastest path to store observability.

## Shorthand And Placeholder Legend

- `<mv>` = memory-viewers ticket store workspace path: `C:/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine/memory-viewers` (pass as `workspace` for all roadmap ticket ops).
- `TRACKER` = ticket `c892904f-6aff-4892-baf1-1f832837cde5` — "Tracker: ticket-viewer store observability roadmap".
- `W1` = ticket `a34d9891-5cd3-403c-8576-f3a55a50047e` — "[ticket-viewer] Consolidate detail panel into content panel (compact header, inline edit, metadata footer)".
- `W2` = ticket `e978d833-bbaa-4cb8-8c1a-52a282a079d9` — "[ticket-viewer] Render bounded neighbourhood subgraph from cached full graph".
- `W3` = ticket `6ccbe0b7-0b6c-44fc-989b-db66c963b623` — "[viewer-api][ticket-viewer] Fix FileTree left-panel formatting and resize/render performance".
- `W4` = ticket `1b0dbe8a-1828-41e7-b33f-4a066c3622bf` — "[ticket-viewer] Clickable parent/child neighbours in content panel with cache-driven graph retarget".
- `W5a` = ticket `0d995e82-26b8-4f71-afc0-f5de75d5a468` — "[viewer-api] Create minimal viewer-template bootstrap".
- `W5b` = ticket `966a7714-0bf4-443b-b778-8e0f383f99d8` — "[viewer-api] Build demo-viewer exercising all viewer components with root-level domain selection".
- `W6` = ticket `e0d8ccef-49d8-4446-bd63-78626a4163dc` — "[viewer-api] Optimize Graph3D renderer for performance and extensibility".
- `W7` = ticket `d893ed4c-a6a2-4fe4-94c5-175d4da13a9b` — "[ticket-mcp][memory-api] Enumerate discovered descendant stores in list_workspaces for viewer domain selection".
- `SPEC-TEMPLATE` = spec `012c2c1c-f33e-45d9-9ad1-aa4fc65ce7b6` — "viewer-api: minimal viewer-template shell" (created this session).
- `SPEC-DETAIL` = spec `8c4d51ef-c0b6-437d-bc14-672b0802cef2` — "ticket-viewer: integrated ticket document and focused full-graph view".
- `SPEC-GRAPHFOCUS` = spec `98b4f75d-3628-470d-a5cc-c91b6cc9811a` — "ticket-viewer: graph focus, property-based rendering, and 2D presentation mode".
- `RULE-NOTE` = rule entry `dfc75fa6-2780-4f7d-8583-2e9aded6cb11` — "Workspace Discovery vs Enumeration".

## Ticket Legend (shorthand -> full UUID + title)

- TRACKER -> `c892904f-6aff-4892-baf1-1f832837cde5` — Tracker: ticket-viewer store observability roadmap
- W1 -> `a34d9891-5cd3-403c-8576-f3a55a50047e` — [ticket-viewer] Consolidate detail panel into content panel (compact header, inline edit, metadata footer)
- W2 -> `e978d833-bbaa-4cb8-8c1a-52a282a079d9` — [ticket-viewer] Render bounded neighbourhood subgraph from cached full graph
- W3 -> `6ccbe0b7-0b6c-44fc-989b-db66c963b623` — [viewer-api][ticket-viewer] Fix FileTree left-panel formatting and resize/render performance
- W4 -> `1b0dbe8a-1828-41e7-b33f-4a066c3622bf` — [ticket-viewer] Clickable parent/child neighbours in content panel with cache-driven graph retarget
- W5a -> `0d995e82-26b8-4f71-afc0-f5de75d5a468` — [viewer-api] Create minimal viewer-template bootstrap
- W5b -> `966a7714-0bf4-443b-b778-8e0f383f99d8` — [viewer-api] Build demo-viewer exercising all viewer components with root-level domain selection
- W6 -> `e0d8ccef-49d8-4446-bd63-78626a4163dc` — [viewer-api] Optimize Graph3D renderer for performance and extensibility
- W7 -> `d893ed4c-a6a2-4fe4-94c5-175d4da13a9b` — [ticket-mcp][memory-api] Enumerate discovered descendant stores in list_workspaces for viewer domain selection
- Superseded/obsoleted (still open, need re-scope): `6e7a15c9-d8e6-4bbe-bb34-b83bd651896b` — [ticket-viewer] Keep full workspace graph visible with focused navigation; `2b3a6e2e-4911-4b33-a3a9-9ace11f26637` — Bug: TicketDetail right panel hardcoded colors — port to theme variables
- Related in-flight to fold in: `10c94251-1c0c-4542-a282-ea3d75a205b5` — [ticket-viewer][viewer-api] Graph focus and 2D presentation follow-up; `322ba030-160c-41d3-8a12-42936ae92858` — [viewer-api][ticket-viewer] Add multi-level graph node detail rendering; `b779c650-0775-4e4f-a692-3eaaa939a910` — demo-viewer: scaffold bin crate + Dioxus SPA; `d1e4ab96-52e1-4b80-a1d7-bfff459d3fac` — [viewer-api] Converge shared Dioxus viewer shells

## Long-Horizon Goal

Make the internal stores (ticket/spec/rule/audit/session) observable through a consistent, performant viewer platform: a readable ticket-viewer with a single consolidated content panel and easy neighbourhood navigation, plus a reusable viewer-template and demo-viewer that generalize the components to new domains. TRACKER is the epic; it depends on W1..W7.

## Findings

- No discovery bug exists. The root policy [./.ticket/workspace-policy.toml](./.ticket/workspace-policy.toml) sets `include_descendants = true`; [./memory-api/crates/memory-api/src/workspace.rs](./memory-api/crates/memory-api/src/workspace.rs) recursively discovers descendant `.ticket` stores. Root index [./.ticket/index.toon](./.ticket/index.toon) already aggregates memory-viewers (83), memory-api (185), viewer-api (92), context-stack (6) tickets. `get_ticket 6e7a15c9 --workspace default` resolves to the real memory-viewers path.
- The confusion was `list_workspaces`, which only enumerates the aggregated root and does NOT list descendant stores as selectable workspaces — presentation limitation, not exclusion. This is the genuine gap captured as W7 (also unblocks the demo-viewer domain selector in W5b).
- Writes matter: creating tickets against `workspace: default` lands them in the root store, so roadmap tickets were created against `<mv>` to co-locate with the ticket-viewer/viewer-api code and E2E ownership.
- Existing viewer specs live in submodule spec stores (e.g. demo-viewer spec physically at [./viewer-api/.spec/specs/4c3b62b4-1198-4ce2-9ef6-df530f38297e](./viewer-api/.spec/specs/4c3b62b4-1198-4ce2-9ef6-df530f38297e)); SPEC-TEMPLATE was created in the `viewer-api` workspace to match.
- SPEC-DETAIL and SPEC-GRAPHFOCUS have empty "Provided Surface Contracts"/"Required Validation" sections (real content only under "Legacy Content (Preserved)").

## Decisions

- Single tracked roadmap in `<mv>`, tracker-improvement type, high priority; TRACKER depends_on all workstreams.
- W2 pivots away from the "keep full graph visible" approach (`6e7a15c9`) to bounded-neighbourhood-from-cache, per the user's explicit direction.
- W1 removes the right details panel entirely, which obsoletes `2b3a6e2e` (theme-color fix on a panel being deleted).
- Workflow note added as rule entry RULE-NOTE and composed into [./.agents/instructions/ticket-system.instructions.md](./.agents/instructions/ticket-system.instructions.md) so future sessions do not repeat the discovery/enumeration confusion.

## Dependency Edges (already created)

- TRACKER depends_on W1, W2, W3, W4, W5a, W5b, W6, W7.
- W4 depends_on W1, W2.
- W5b depends_on W5a, W7.
- W6 depends_on W2.

## Blockers

- Uncommitted changes exist in the context-engine repo (RULE-NOTE store files, [./rule-targets/50-agents-instructions.yaml](./rule-targets/50-agents-instructions.yaml), regenerated [./.agents/instructions/ticket-system.instructions.md](./.agents/instructions/ticket-system.instructions.md)) and in the `memory-viewers` submodule ticket store (9 new tickets + edges). Not yet committed.
- `6e7a15c9-d8e6-4bbe-bb34-b83bd651896b` and `2b3a6e2e-4911-4b33-a3a9-9ace11f26637` are still open and not linked/re-scoped against TRACKER, so the graph does not yet reflect supersession.
- No test-mcp validation specs recorded for the workstreams yet.
- Scratch file [./target/tmp/workspace-discovery-note.md](./target/tmp/workspace-discovery-note.md) is ephemeral (rule body already persisted); do not commit it.

## Key Entities

- [TRACKER ticket](./memory-viewers/.ticket/tickets/c892904f-6aff-4892-baf1-1f832837cde5/ticket.toml) — epic to resume from; run `next` scoped to it.
- [W1](./memory-viewers/.ticket/tickets/a34d9891-5cd3-403c-8576-f3a55a50047e/ticket.toml), [W2](./memory-viewers/.ticket/tickets/e978d833-bbaa-4cb8-8c1a-52a282a079d9/ticket.toml), [W3](./memory-viewers/.ticket/tickets/6ccbe0b7-0b6c-44fc-989b-db66c963b623/ticket.toml) — P0 leaves, unblocked, start here.
- [W4](./memory-viewers/.ticket/tickets/1b0dbe8a-1828-41e7-b33f-4a066c3622bf/ticket.toml), [W6](./memory-viewers/.ticket/tickets/e0d8ccef-49d8-4446-bd63-78626a4163dc/ticket.toml) — P1, depend on W1/W2.
- [W5a](./memory-viewers/.ticket/tickets/0d995e82-26b8-4f71-afc0-f5de75d5a468/ticket.toml), [W5b](./memory-viewers/.ticket/tickets/966a7714-0bf4-443b-b778-8e0f383f99d8/ticket.toml), [W7](./memory-viewers/.ticket/tickets/d893ed4c-a6a2-4fe4-94c5-175d4da13a9b/ticket.toml) — P2 platform generalization + enumeration.
- [SPEC-TEMPLATE](./viewer-api/.spec/specs/012c2c1c-f33e-45d9-9ad1-aa4fc65ce7b6) — new viewer-template contract for W5a.
- [SPEC-DETAIL](./.spec) id `8c4d51ef-c0b6-437d-bc14-672b0802cef2` and [SPEC-GRAPHFOCUS](./.spec) id `98b4f75d-3628-470d-a5cc-c91b6cc9811a` — need contract sections filled (fetch via spec-mcp/spec-cli with those ids).
- [RULE-NOTE body](./.rule/rules/dfc75fa6-2780-4f7d-8583-2e9aded6cb11/body.md) — durable workspace discovery-vs-enumeration note.
- [discovery source](./memory-api/crates/memory-api/src/workspace.rs) — implement W7 by extending list_workspaces to enumerate `discover_workspace_scan_roots_with_policy` results.
- Workflow refs: [./AGENTS.md](./AGENTS.md), [./.agents/instructions/ticket-system.instructions.md](./.agents/instructions/ticket-system.instructions.md), [./.agents/instructions/spec-system.instructions.md](./.agents/instructions/spec-system.instructions.md).

## Next Actions (execute in order)

1. Re-scope supersession: link `6e7a15c9-d8e6-4bbe-bb34-b83bd651896b` and `2b3a6e2e-4911-4b33-a3a9-9ace11f26637` under TRACKER (add depends_on/related edges), then `cancel` each with a reason pointing at W2 and W1 respectively. Workspace `<mv>`.
2. Fill SPEC-DETAIL (`8c4d51ef-...`) and SPEC-GRAPHFOCUS (`98b4f75d-...`) "Provided Surface Contracts" and "Required Validation" sections from their Legacy content and the W1/W2/W4/W6 acceptance criteria.
3. Record test-mcp validation specs for W1..W7 and link ticket_ids so evidence is traceable.
4. Commit deepest-first: (a) memory-viewers submodule ticket store, then bump submodule pointer; (b) context-engine repo: RULE-NOTE store + [./rule-targets/50-agents-instructions.yaml](./rule-targets/50-agents-instructions.yaml) + regenerated [./.agents/instructions/ticket-system.instructions.md](./.agents/instructions/ticket-system.instructions.md). Do NOT commit [./target/tmp/workspace-discovery-note.md](./target/tmp/workspace-discovery-note.md). Pre-commit hook runs `rule sync-targets --check`.
5. Begin implementation: `ticket next c892904f-6aff-4892-baf1-1f832837cde5 --workspace <mv>` to confirm the leaf frontier, board check-in, and start with W3 (self-contained FileTree perf) or W1 (content-panel consolidation).

First concrete action: run action 1 (link + cancel `6e7a15c9-...` and `2b3a6e2e-...`).
First validation check: `ticket health c892904f-6aff-4892-baf1-1f832837cde5 --workspace <mv>` returns no cycles/dangling and no missing-effort warnings.

## Completion Status

- Action 1 (re-scope superseded tickets): not-started.
- Action 2 (fill spec contract sections): not-started.
- Action 3 (record validation specs): not-started.
- Action 4 (commit deepest-first): not-started — working tree dirty in root repo + memory-viewers submodule.
- Action 5 (begin implementation at P0 leaf): not-started — no board check-in yet.

## Definition Of Done

- `6e7a15c9-...` and `2b3a6e2e-...` cancelled and linked to TRACKER; graph reflects supersession.
- SPEC-DETAIL and SPEC-GRAPHFOCUS have concrete provided-contract + required-validation sections referencing the workstream tickets.
- Validation specs recorded and linked for the workstreams.
- Roadmap changes committed deepest-first with clean pre-commit; submodule pointer bumped.
- At least the P0 leaf (W3 or W1) is checked in on the board and under active implementation.

## Board / Session Note

No active board check-in exists for this roadmap track; check in against the chosen P0 leaf before editing viewer files. The `default` vs `<mv>` write distinction (see RULE-NOTE) is the single most important thing to preserve: always pass `<mv>` for roadmap ticket writes.

Execute the next actions in order and continue until all are completed or explicitly blocked.

 ## Provided Surface Contracts
 
-- Define provided contracts for this behavior slice.
+- The ticket-viewer main reading surface is a single content panel that contains the selected ticket title, editable fields/actions, summary metadata, and contextual content in one document flow.
+- The separate right-side detail panel is removed from the default experience; features previously only available there are preserved inside the content panel.
+- The content-panel header remains compact and avoids full-width preface blocks before the primary body.
+- Non-essential metadata is rendered in a footer section so the core document remains scannable.
+- The content panel exposes immediate parent and child neighbours for the selected ticket.
+- Parent/child neighbour items are clickable and retarget the active selection.
+- Neighbour retargeting updates graph focus from client-side cached graph data without per-click graph refetch.
+- Graph selection and neighbour-driven selection stay behaviorally consistent (same selected ticket, same focus retarget semantics).
 
 ## Required Validation
 
-- Triangulate behavior with executable checks, natural-language clauses, and code/schema/API references when available.
+- Playwright release coverage validates that default ticket reading uses one consolidated content panel and no standalone right detail panel is required for core read/edit flows.
+- Playwright release coverage validates compact header behavior and metadata footer placement for selected tickets with representative field density.
+- Playwright release coverage validates parity of actions/edit capabilities between the pre-change detail experience and the consolidated panel (no functional regressions).
+- Playwright release coverage validates parent/child neighbour lists in the content panel and confirms each neighbour click retargets selection to the expected ticket.
+- Playwright release coverage validates neighbour-click retargeting reuses cached graph data and does not issue a network fetch for per-click graph expansion.
+- Focused browser validation in external Chromium confirms navigation clarity, readability, and retarget consistency in both split and content-only layouts.
+- Ticket traceability for this spec is maintained through W1 `a34d9891-5cd3-403c-8576-f3a55a50047e` and W4 `1b0dbe8a-1828-41e7-b33f-4a066c3622bf`.
*** End Patch

 ## Provided Surface Contracts
 
-- Define provided contracts for this behavior slice.
+- Ticket-viewer graph data flow supports loading a workspace-scale graph into a client-side cache while rendering only a bounded neighbourhood around the active selection.
+- Bounded neighbourhood rendering is parameterized by configurable graph-distance/focus rules and avoids full-graph visual overload.
+- Selection changes recompute the rendered neighbourhood from cache without network refetch.
+- Graph focus behavior preserves predictable selection semantics: selected node remains emphasized, immediate neighbours retain higher emphasis, and low-relevance nodes degrade by explicit focus bands.
+- Node rendering follows an ordered level-of-detail ladder (`point/sphere -> icon -> label -> compact -> full`) driven by projected size, focus, and visibility budget.
+- The shared Graph3D renderer remains extensible and performant for focus, LOD tiers, and cache-bounded neighbourhood rendering, with reusable extension points for additional viewers.
+- Optional fixed 2D presentation mode remains available with matching 2D framing behavior and consistent selection/focus expectations.
 
 ## Required Validation
 
-- Triangulate behavior with executable checks, natural-language clauses, and code/schema/API references when available.
+- Playwright release coverage validates bounded neighbourhood rendering from cached full-graph data and confirms selection changes do not trigger graph refetches.
+- Playwright release coverage validates focus-band behavior (selected node, immediate neighbours, wider context, low-focus falloff) and outside-click deselection behavior.
+- Playwright release coverage validates ordered LOD transitions, including one-tier hover promotion and camera-mode-appropriate low-tier glyphs.
+- Playwright release coverage validates optional fixed 2D mode switching and parity of core focus/selection interactions between 2D and 3D modes.
+- Focused renderer performance validation documents graph-size baselines, frame stability, and regression thresholds for cache-bounded neighbourhood updates.
+- Extension-point documentation validation confirms renderer integration points remain consumable for non-ticket viewers (rule/audit/demo).
+- Ticket traceability for this spec is maintained through W2 `e978d833-bbaa-4cb8-8c1a-52a282a079d9` and W6 `e0d8ccef-49d8-4446-bd63-78626a4163dc`.
*** End Patch
